subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear overall logical flow with a defined introduction, body, and conclusion
Effective use of headings, sub-headings, and table of contents to guide the reader
Succinct and engaging title that frames the investigationâs focus
weaknesses: Occasional numbering errors in figures and inconsistent page references disrupt continuity
Verbosity in algebraic expansions reduces conciseness
Incomplete placeholders (e.g. missing code identifier) detract from formality
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strands in coherence and organization both sit solidly at band 3, while conciseness is at band 2. Overall, the work is well structured and largely coherent, with only minor lapses preventing an exceptional rating, warranting 3 / 4.

strengths: Use of GeoGebra plots, polynomial fits, and diagrams supports understanding
Stepwise presentation of Riemann sums to integrals shows logical linkage
weaknesses: Several mis-copied expressions and missing notation elements (e.g. âdxâ) hamper clarity
Logical clarity fluctuates across complex manipulations
Key algebraic transitions are not always explained, reducing reader accessibility
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although notation use and representation are generally effective (band 3), inconsistencies in notation, missing differentials, and fluctuating clarity in logical structure align more closely with the descriptor for band 2, resulting in 2 / 4.

strengths: Evidence of independent thinking in deriving the SA formula rather than quoting it
Personal context (wine-glass geometry) informs modelling choices
Use of extensions (fuel-cells, radiators) demonstrates initiative
weaknesses: Approach remains within standard calculus methods without highly innovative twists
Personal reflections are present but not sustained throughout
Creativity is good but not exceptional
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of independent thinking, personal approach, and creativity map to band 2. The work shows clear personal engagement and initiative, but does not reach the outstanding level required for band 3, meriting 2 / 3.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on methodological strengths, limitations, and extensions
Clear evaluation of glass outcomes and broader implications
weaknesses: Critical analysis of mathematical choices is basic and not deeply explored
No quantified assessment of model error or uncertainty
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection and evaluation strands align with band 2, but the critical analysis remains at band 1. On balance, the criterion aligns with the overall descriptor for band 2, yielding 2 / 3.

strengths: Appropriate and effective application of calculus to solids of revolution across multiple cases
Mathematics is fully commensurate with the course level and applied without major conceptual gaps
Generally correct integrations and derivatives with minor transcription errors only
weaknesses: A mis-bracketed integrand and one unresolved simplification indicate room for precision improvement
Occasional minor algebraic or notation slips do not affect final conclusions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All strandsârelevance, level appropriateness, and understandingâcorrespond to band 4: mathematics is used effectively and at the correct level with good accuracy. Therefore, the criterion score is 4 / 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Calculations are shown in a stepwise manner, but several expressions are mis-copied and some simplifications are hard to follow.  Logical links exist yet clarity fluctuates, so only âsome logical structure and clarityâ is fully met.

Derivatives and integrals are generally correct; minor transcription errors and one unresolved simplification do not materially affect final results.  Explanations indicate solid conceptual grasp, aligning with âgood understanding and mostly correctâ.

Functions are displayed graphically, 3-D solids of revolution are shown, and results are summarised in a table.  These representations complement one another and aid understanding, so use is effective, though not refined enough for âhighly effectiveâ.

The investigation follows a clear sequence: introduction, derivation of formulae, modelling of each glass, conclusion and evaluation.  Ideas generally build on one another without abrupt gaps, so the reader can follow the argument, although occasional numbering errors and duplicated figure labels interrupt the flow.  Overall coherence is solid but not exemplary.

Selecting GeoGebra for curve-fitting, constructing 3-D rotations and performing lengthy symbolic integrals demonstrates initiative beyond textbook examples.  Creativity is good, though modelling remains within familiar calculus territory, so the highest band is not justified.

The student comments on why the Bordeaux glass gives higher aeration and links this to wine practice, but analysis of mathematical results or modelling validity is basic and not deeply critical.  Hence only the âbasic analysisâ descriptor is met.

Outcomes are compared, and limitations concerning open tops and sample size are acknowledged.  Possible extensions to fuel-cells and radiators indicate awareness of broader implications, constituting a clear evaluation, though not comprehensive enough for the top band.

Reflection is present in the evaluation section, discussing methodological strengths, limitations and potential extensions.  Commentary is meaningful but relatively brief and placed at the end rather than woven throughout, fulfilling âmeaningful reflectionâ.

Mathematical notation for integrals, limits and derivatives is generally correct and used consistently, though a few bracket and denominator slips appear.  The overall use is appropriate and predominantly accurate, satisfying the âmostly consistentâ descriptor.

The studentâs personal interest in wine-glass geometry drives the exploration, and modelling choices (piecewise, logarithmic fits) stem from this personal approach, giving clear evidence but not highly innovative treatment.

Most material is relevant to modelling surface-area/volume ratios, yet long verbatim algebraic expansions and an extended derivation of the surface-area integral add length without advancing the investigation.  These unnecessary details reduce conciseness; hence only âmostly relevantâ is achieved.

Calculus of solids of revolution, piecewise and logarithmic fitting, and numerical integrations are all directly relevant to determining SA/V ratios, and are applied appropriately across three casesâmeeting the ârelevant mathematics used effectivelyâ band.

Choosing an everyday context and constructing original models shows significant independent thought, especially the decision to derive the surface-area formula rather than quote it.  However, methodology largely follows standard calculus techniques, so evidence is not outstanding.

Headings, sub-headings and a table of contents give the work a recognisable structure with introduction, body and conclusion.  Each glass is tackled in a parallel format.  Minor inconsistencies (page references, repeated figure numbers) prevent the âexceptionalâ standard, but overall organization is clear and purposeful.

Use of definite integrals, differentiation, and curve-fitting is fully commensurate with HL AA level.  The complexity is appropriate and sustained, but not unusually sophisticated to merit 5 or 6 marks.

annotations.comment: The introduction paragraph effectively conveys personal motivation, but could be broken into shorter sentences for enhanced clarity and cohesion.
Contextual linkage to catalytic converters shows thoughtful extension beyond pure mathematics, reflecting genuine personal engagement.
Figure 1 and its caption numbering are duplicated later; renumber sequentially (e.g., Figure 1a, 1b) to maintain clarity in references.
A brief restatement of the Mean Value Theoremâs hypotheses before its use would reinforce understanding of the transition from finite sums to differential expressions.
The step from Riemann sums to the definite integral is logically presented, demonstrating clear mathematical communication and structure.
The discussion of limits as $n\to\infty$ could include reflection on how choosing $x_i^*$ and $x_i^{**}$ affects convergence, deepening critical analysis.
The GeoGebra plot and polynomial fit for Glass 1 demonstrate effective use of multiple representations and support the modelling process.
The title succinctly outlines the investigationâs focus and context, providing a clear and engaging introduction to the exploration.
The placeholder for candidate code is left blank; include your code or identifier to meet formal requirements and ensure proper authentication.
Entries in the table of contents show inconsistent page numbering (e.g., âExplorationâ vs âGlass 1â), which disrupts navigation; verify and correct each reference.
The aim and rationale are clearly stated, linking surface-area/volume ratio to oxidation; this provides a strong conceptual foundation.
Equation (1) omits the differential element âdxâ; include it to adhere to standard integral notation and avoid ambiguity.
The diagram illustrating frustum approximation effectively supports the derivation, aiding visual comprehension of the method.
When introducing the surface-area integral, explicitly note the factor $2\pi$ earlier to streamline the readerâs understanding of its origin.
Equation (7) is missing a closing bracket around the square-root term, which can mislead; check and correct the notation for consistency.
Detail the input commands or steps used on the TI-Nspire to improve reproducibility of the surface-area calculation.
The innovative use of piecewise functions to model different regions of the Sauvignon Blanc glass reveals clear independent thinking.
The boundary $x=0.235$ for the piecewise fit is stated without justification or discussion of fitting error; reflect on how this choice affects model accuracy.
Consider summarising large polynomial expansions or relocating them to an appendix to preserve flow and improve conciseness.
The extensions section ends mid-sentence regarding radiators; complete the thought to fully explore future applications.
Include units (cm) when defining the function $f(x)$ and its domain to clarify the scale and context for subsequent integrations.
The integrand in the volume formula is mis-bracketed, implying a division by $(1.5799x+0.3763)$ instead of squaring the entire polynomial; revise the parentheses.
The integration result is rounded clearly to two decimal places and units are stated, enhancing the credibility and precision of the final value.
Provide a brief overview of key algebraic manipulations between major steps to help readers follow complex multi-term integrations.
Efficient use of the graphing calculator (TI-Nspire CX II CAS) to evaluate challenging integrals demonstrates competent application of technology.
There is no quantified assessment of error or uncertainty in the calculated volumes, limiting critical evaluation of model accuracy.
Interval splitting for surface area aligns well with the piecewise model and reflects appropriate application of integration techniques.
The function expression on page 13 for the second interval is misprinted with a denominator bar; ensure the correct polynomial form is used.
The combined SA/V ratio results are presented clearly in Table 1, enabling easy comparison of the three glass designs.
The conclusionâs citation (âMarshall Tilden III, 2024â) lacks a full reference in the bibliography; include complete details to support credibility.
Reflection on the assumption that glasses are perfect solids of revolution is noted only at the end; weave critical commentary on modelling validity throughout.
Identifying fuel cells and radiators as potential applications extends the investigation thoughtfully beyond wine glasses, demonstrating initiative.
